canonical_question: How do I implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)?
aliases: how to add roles and permissions
constraints: Always enforce authorization server-side; never rely on client-side role checks alone
Default to deny — every route/resource must explicitly grant access, not explicitly deny
Separate authentication from authorization — verify identity first, then check permissions
Check permissions not roles — code should test 'can user do X?' not 'is user admin?'
Principle of least privilege — assign the minimum permissions required for each role

skip_this_unit_if.condition: You need attribute-based fine-grained access (time-of-day, IP, resource owner)
You need object-level ownership checks (user can only edit their own posts)
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Relationship-Based Access Control (ReBAC) or combine RBAC with ownership checks

inputs_needed.question: What web framework are you using?
Which RBAC model complexity do you need?
options: hierarchical (RBAC1 - roles inherit from parent roles)

inputs_needed.question: Where will you store roles and permissions?
